ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Cabbage Poriyal; closest defensible match is cooked cabbage food product (FOODON_03315671). Poriyal-specific preparation not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Cabbage, cooked) is the closest defensible match. Poriyal preparation with coconut and tempering spices is not individually coded in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Cabbage Poriyal or equivalent South Indian cabbage stir-fry preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Cabbage Poriyal is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a medium-sized side-dish portion served alongside rice and sambar in a South Indian meal.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods for cabbage (cooked) and freshly grated coconut, combined in proportions consistent with a standard Cabbage Poriyal recipe. Values for the composite dish are estimated from component data. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-reference for coconut and mustard seed contributions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Cabbage Poriyal as a composite dish. GI estimated at 15 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked cabbage (GI ~15, low category). The addition of coconut fat and cooking oil further slows gastric emptying, supporting a low GI classification. GL per 100 g serving calculated as (15 × 7.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (150 mg/100 g) with beneficial potassium (210 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Cabbage Poriyal is generally suitable for individuals with hypertension; however, added salt should be minimised or omitted during preparation to keep sodium intake within recommended limits (less than 2000 mg/day).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~15) and low available carbohydrate (7.5 g/100 g) with 3.5 g dietary fibre supporting attenuated postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cabbage Poriyal is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; its very low GI, high fibre content, and modest carbohydrate load contribute to improved glycaemic control when consumed as part of a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat primarily from coconut (~4.5 g/100 g) is notable; however, the dish is cholesterol-free, fibre-rich, and contains beneficial phytonutrients from cabbage
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cabbage Poriyal can be included in a heart-healthy diet in moderate portions; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the saturated fat contribution from coconut and limit portion size accordingly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Cabbage contains sulforaphane and glucosinolates with potential anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Cabbage Poriyal may be beneficial for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the anti-inflammatory compounds in cabbage; cooking reduces goitrogenic activity, making it safe for regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cabbage Poriyal (also known as Muttaikose Poriyal in Tamil and Elekosu Palya in Kannada) is a classic South Indian dry vegetable stir-fry made from finely shredded cabbage. The dish is prepared by tempering mustard seeds, urad dal, curry leaves, and green chilies in oil, then adding shredded cabbage and stir-frying until just tender but still retaining a light crunch. It is finished with a generous amount of freshly grated coconut, which imparts a mildly sweet, nutty flavour and a soft texture contrast. The overall taste profile is mildly spiced, slightly sweet from the cabbage and coconut, with aromatic notes from the curry leaves and mustard seeds. Cabbage Poriyal is a staple side dish in Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Kerala households, typically served alongside steamed rice, sambar, and rasam as part of a traditional South Indian thali. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 95 kcal per 100 g, with 2.2 g protein, 6.8 g fat (predominantly saturated fat from coconut), and 7.5 g carbohydrate. It is a good source of dietary fibre (3.5 g), vitamin C (30 mg), vitamin K (75 mcg), and folate (45 mcg). Its very low glycemic index (~15) and low glycemic load make it particularly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or seeking blood sugar control. The dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Individuals with thyroid conditions should note that cabbage is a mild goitrogen, though cooking substantially reduces this effect.
common_user_queries: Is Cabbage Poriyal good for diabetes?
Can I eat Cabbage Poriyal if I have hypertension?
meal_context: Part of a traditional South Indian thali

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN component data for cooked cabbage and coconut rather than a direct measurement of the composite Cabbage Poriyal dish. No single approved-source entry exists for this exact preparation. Values are internally consistent and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for coconut and mustard seed contributions. GI is an analogous estimate based on cooked cabbage from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): Cabbage Poriyal is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero for this vegan preparation, confirmed by ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): No vitamin D-containing ingredients are present; confirmed zero for plant-based preparations per ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: No hydrogenated fats used; confirmed negligible for traditional home-prepared poriyal. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: All ingredients are plant-derived; cholesterol is naturally zero. 5. water_g is set to -1: Moisture content for the composite cooked Cabbage Poriyal dish is not directly reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation; both sources were consulted and neither provides a moisture figure for this composite dish as of 2024. 6. saturated_fat_g (4.5 g) is estimated from coconut contribution (~30 g freshly grated coconut per 100 g dish); monounsaturated_fat_g (0.8 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy coconut and oil data. 7. Cabbage is a mild goitrogen; cooking substantially reduces goitrogenic activity. Individuals with thyroid conditions should consult a healthcare provider. 8. Portions in the input JSON contained three identical duplicate entries (100 g each); deduplicated to a single 100 g serving for output.
